rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ELLIOTT H WHEELER TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #57, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Court to determine whether proposed distribution to charities should be granted. 2) Court to determine whether the $150k reserve requested is reasonable. 3) Trustees request $73,003.52, which is based on .75% of the FMV. a. Trustees request $34,726.53 for the 2020 account period. i. $16,740.26 was paid to Dodge & Cox for investment advisor services provided during the 2020 accounting period. b. Trustees request $38,276.98 for the 2021 account period. Trustees have clarified that, although the 2021 accounting ends 6/30/21, their fee request includes services provided through the end of trust administration (see supplement filed on 11/19/21). i. $10,195.15 was paid to Dodge & Cox for investment advisor services provided during the first two quarters of 2021. It is unknown how much was/will be paid for the remaining two quarters. c. Trustees allege that a portion of the $19,994 paid to Sutter Hill during the 3rd quarter of 2020 was for the preparation of the 56th account. Trustees do no specify the exact amount paid to prepare the accounting. Trustees also do not allege how much was paid to Sutter Hill to prepare the instant 57th & final account. 4) Court to determine reasonableness of request for attorneys' fee of $52,881 ($775-$1,300/hr; $430/hr for paralegal). Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 10th amended general order.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF WAH LUM HONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO TERMINATE CONSERVATORSHIP of Person and Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The conservatorship estate held 3 annuities in the total market value of $58,012.56. The conservator reported all the annuities as assets on hand at the end of this final account period. Nevertheless, the conservator requests an order "relieving her of liability for the distribution of the conservatee's annuity interests". Probate Code section 2467(a) provides that the conservator continues to have the duty of custody and conservation of the estate after the death of the conservatee pending the delivery thereof to the personal representative of the conservatee's estate or other disposition according to law. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ELIJAH MIMS, JR., AKA ELIJAH MIMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1. Petitioner requests $1,620 as extraordinary fees. Rather than timesheets, Petitioner filed a declaration on 6/18/21 providing an estimated number of hours spent on ordinary services, including 245 hours for correspondence, 100 hours of travel, and 75 hours of document review. It is unclear how these estimates were obtained. 2. The Salinas Law Group requests $3,480 for time spent to secure bond waivers from intestate heirs, including from a client of Golden Gate Regional Center. Attorney alleges this was required due to appointed administrators not be able to be bonded. It is noted that obtaining waiver of bonds is usually considered ordinary services. 3. The Salinas Law Group requests $10,980.00 to appoint Delvin Curry as the sole personal representative, which included filing a petition for his "reappointment" and a subsequent petition to remove, upon instructions from the court. 4. Current attorney's request for extraordinary fees includes many instances of travel time for travel to court and to banks. 5. Regarding the 13101 Affidavit re: Michael Mims: Per PC 13006, "successor of the decedent" is defined as the person(s) who succeeded to the property of the decedent under PC 6401 and 6402. Thus, the successor in interest to the Estates of Elijah Mims III and James Mims cannot be the successors in interest of Michael Mims. It is also noted that PC 13006 defines "successor of the decedent" as all of the persons entitled to the property. Thus, it appears that all of the successors in interest must sign the affidavit for property of Michael Mims to pass via 13101 Affidavit, which appears impossible here. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 10th amended general order.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DAWN MARIE CAMERON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The executor paid the attorney's fees and costs in the total amount of $2,559.25 from this estate without prior court approval. Such fees and costs were incurred for the attorney's services associated with the petition for instructions filed in the matter of the decedent's parent's estate for the purpose of establishing a special needs trust for the decedent. The executor also paid $34.50 as a "personal" expense from this estate. 2) The decedent left a holographic will stating that: "I leave the entirety of my estate, any and all monies in care of my brother Keith Cameron for the benefit of my nephew Titus McCoy Cameron." Titus is 3 years old. The petition alleges that the estate cash will be held in a blocked account until Titus reaches 18 years of age. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For An Order Finding And Correcting Scrivners Error In The Trust And For Confirmation Of Assets In The Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/26/2022. The 1994 Grant Deed shows the real property held as "Queen Ester Anderson, a Married Woman, as her sole and separate property and Joanne Anderson, an un married woman". Although there is an affidavit Death of Joint Tenant filed in 2007 the real property does not appear it was held in joint tenancy according to the 1994 Deed and it appears it was held as tenants in common. Please explain the legal basis/legal authority for ownership of the property. It appears if there are other issue/heirs of Queen Ester Anderson they may be entitled to an interest in the real property and need to receive 30 days' notice of the petition. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 1/18/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Trustee'S First Account
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required for counsel to explain: the final I & A did not include the right to inherit from the two related estates (see page 24 in the probate referee handbook); and the first account was due 11/16/21 and has not been filed. counsel may be asked to explain the delay and discuss whether the trustee is ready to file. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN COLIN CAMPBELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/18/2022. 1) The court will not grant preliminary distribution of the real property at this time. Counsel may amend to only request preliminary distribution of the pecuniary gifts and the petition must not include an account but should include a status date for filing the first account. 2) There is no such filing as a partial account and the petition must be amended. 3) The court will not approve an account that shows actions taken by Lolly Roman Sangster to manage estate property when there appears to be no legal basis for her to act on behalf of the estate. 4) When the account is filed please explain the legal basis for Schedule D Lolly Sangster Reimbursement for real property appraisals of $28,000.00 when the real property was appraised by the probate referee. 5) When the account is filed please explain 8/19/2021 Assets inventory expense for $3,163.37 and file a receipt. 6) When the account is filed any request for reimbursement from the estate must be substantiated. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 1/3/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ELWOOD GEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for the Court to determine if the matter should be taken off calendar for failure to file all the necessary papers to meet all statutory requirements. Petitioner is wasting the Court's resources. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDDA CABRERA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/24/2022. Second continuance for same issues. 1) There is no publication on file for the amended petition. (See CRC 7.53 and Probate Code 8121.) 2) Petitioner has not filed or given notice on DE-121. (Probate Code 8100) 3) Petitioner did not check required boxes on the petition page 3 #5a. (7) or (8). 4) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form.[LRSF 14.18 E.] 5) Examiner received unfiled courtesy copies that have been recycled. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 6) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 01/10/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
